name: Cee - Cape Finisterre Return
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cee are characterized by the rugged Costa da Morte coastline and the varied terrain of Galicia. The region features dramatic cliffs, white sand beaches, and estuaries, with routes often following sections of the historic Camino Finisterre. Cyclists can expect a mix of coastal paths and inland trails that traverse rolling hills and offer views of the Atlantic Ocean. The landscape includes significant elevation changes, providing challenging climbs and rewarding descents. Best touring cycling routes around Cee The most popular touring cycling route is Cee - Cape Finisterre Return, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route offers dramatic coastal scenery as it leads towards the iconic Cape Finisterre. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Port of Pindo – San Pedro Beach, O Pindo loop from Cee, a difficult 48.9 miles (78.7 km) path. This route provides extensive coastal views and passes by the base of Mount Pindo. Local touring cyclists also love the Fisterra lighthouse cross – Fisterra Port loop from Cee, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail leading through coastal villages and offering views of the Fisterra lighthouse, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Cee is defined by its dramatic coastline, estuaries like Ría de Lires, and the historic Camino Finisterre. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Cee are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cee's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cee?
A: Cee offers over a dozen touring cycling routes, providing a variety of options for exploring the rugged Costa da Morte. The komoot community has mapped 13 distinct routes in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cee?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Cee is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and more stable. Summer can also be pleasant, though it may be busier with tourists. Winter can bring more unpredictable weather, but the dramatic coastal scenery remains impressive.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Cee?
A: While many routes around Cee feature significant elevation changes and are rated as difficult, there are a couple of moderate options available. These routes offer a less strenuous experience, allowing families or less experienced cyclists to enjoy the beautiful coastal scenery without tackling the most challenging climbs.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on touring cycling routes around Cee?
A: Touring cycling routes in Cee are characterized by the varied terrain of Galicia, often featuring significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955843753/cee-cape-finisterre-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cee - Cape Finisterre Return&lt;/a&gt; route involves over 770 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Boat – Fisterra lighthouse cross loop from Cee&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1300 meters of climbing, providing challenging rides for those seeking them.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Cee?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Cee are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24606696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Pindo – San Pedro Beach, O Pindo loop from Cee&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive coastal views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisterra lighthouse cross – Fisterra Port loop from Cee&lt;/a&gt;, leading through coastal villages.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while cycling around Cee?
A: The region boasts dramatic natural beauty. You can cycle past stunning beaches like Praia da Concha, Estorde Beach, and Praia de Gures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3859881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moa Summit (Monte Pindo)&lt;/a&gt; provides a dramatic backdrop, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4803815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, where the Xallas River plunges directly into the Atlantic, is a must-see natural monument.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Cee and its surroundings are rich in history. Many routes follow sections of the historic Camino Finisterre, leading to landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/218305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisterra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/261550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kilometre Zero Marker, Cape Finisterre&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the Church of Santa María de Xunqueira, the Port of Cee, or the Toba mills, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: Can I visit the Ézaro Waterfall by bike?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4803815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is accessible by bike and is a popular destination. It's located south of Cee and is a rare natural phenomenon where a river plunges directly into the ocean. Cycling to it offers a rewarding experience with scenic coastal views along the way.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cee?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cee are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cee's varied terrain, often praising the dramatic coastal scenery, the challenging climbs, and the unique experience of following sections of the Camino Finisterre.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Camino Finisterre?
A: Yes, Cee is a significant stop on the Camino Finisterre, an extension of the Camino de Santiago. Many touring cycling routes in the area incorporate sections of this historic pilgrimage path, offering cyclists a unique blend of cultural heritage and stunning coastal landscapes as they head towards 'the End of the World'.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: The entire Costa da Morte coastline offers numerous scenic viewpoints. Routes often traverse dramatic cliffs and provide panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and estuaries like Ría de Lires. The climb towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/218305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisterra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the base of Mount Pindo are particularly rewarding for breathtaking vistas.

Q: What about parking for touring cyclists in Cee?
A: Cee, as a coastal town and a stop on the Camino, generally offers various parking options, including public parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage for specific regulations and availability, especially during peak season, but finding a suitable spot to start your cycling tour should not be an issue.
tips:
Corcubión is located on a small peninsula which, with a rectangular shape and surrounded by the municipality of Cee, invades the Atlantic Ocean between the Bay of Estorde and its estuary. In this small area, the highest reliefs are about 150 and 200 meters high, located in the center of the peninsula, from where they descend to the sea. On the hill opposite noon, sheltered from the wind and storms, stretches the town of Corcubión.

The coast is basically rocky, with the exception of the part corresponding to the estuary which begins on this side at the Cape of Cee and where, after a sector of high coast where the castle of the Cardinal is located, is the beach of Quenxe and then the port of the city.
